exits.label: ◂ back through the thinning light, to the Wall and the cold

npcs.name: Sister Auria
npcs.title: a warden of the dawn, troubled by the cold thing at her door
npcs.hail: *(The light here is wrong in the most wonderful way — warm, gold, *living*, the first warmth your dead-touched skin has felt since you crossed over, and it makes the cold in you ache like a thawing limb. A woman in dawn-yellow turns from the candles she is tending, and her face does something complicated: gladness at a visitor, then a flinch, because you bring the cold of the Wall into a house built to keep it out.)* …oh. …*oh*, you're — *(she steadies)* …you're the *cold* kind, aren't you. the *Wall's* own *chill* on you. …i'll not turn you *out*, friend; the *Dawn* turns *no* one out, that's the *whole* of the *creed.* …but you should *know* you're the *first* of your *sort* to *ever* stand in this *light.* …*(gently, warily)* …what does a soul the *grey* *kept* …*want* …in the *gold*?

npcs.name: Anwen
npcs.title: a daughter, warm and whole, and wrong about her mother
npcs.hail: *(A young woman sits in a patch of the warmest light, weaving dawn-flowers into a chain with the unhurried ease of someone who has all of a kind eternity and no grief to outrun. She is perhaps fourteen forever, bright and unbroken, exactly the warm whole child a mother three worlds away climbed into a Wall to make. She looks up as your cold reaches her, and where Auria flinched, she only tilts her head, curious, fearless — a soul that has never once been given a reason to be afraid.)* …you're *cold.* *(not an accusation — an observation, almost delighted)* …i didn't know a *cold* thing could come *here.* …*(she sets the chain down)* …you've the look of someone *carrying* something, cold one. people who carry things always look at me like *that.* …like there's a *word* in their *mouth* they're not sure they're *allowed* to *say.*

conversations.title: Sister Auria — the warden of the dawn

variants.text: *(She marks the rose-and-dawn sigil on you and her wariness breaks into open wonder.)* …*Lathander's* *own* — and *cold* — and *here.* …*(she presses a hand to her heart)* …then the *creed* is *truer* than even we *teach* it, friend. the *Dawn* claimed you, and the *Wall* *took* you *anyway*, and *still* you found your *way* *home* to the *light.* …*sit.* whatever the *grey* did to you, you're *kin* under this *roof.* …and i think — by the *weight* on you — you've not come for *rest.* you've come to *give* something. …what is it?

*(Her sense, gentler than yours but real, finds no god's mark on you at all, and her whole careful theology trembles on its hinge.)* …*Faithless.* …and yet the *Dawn* let you *in.* *(she says it like a woman watching her own creed prove itself in a way that frightens her)* …they *teach* us the *unclaimed* can't *cross* the *threshold* of a *god's* *house.* …and here you *stand* in the *gold*, dripping the *Wall's* *cold*, and the *light* hasn't *thrown* you *out.* …*(quietly, and you watch a small good crack open in a wall of doctrine)* …maybe the *light* knows something the *creed* *forgot.* …*tell* me why you've *come*, unclaimed one. i find i *want* to *know.*

*(She studies the cold coming off you, and the dawn-warden's training wars with the dawn-warden's kindness, and the kindness wins, the way it is built to.)* …well. …*cold* or not, you're *here*, and the *Dawn* *turns* *no* one *out.* …*(she folds her hands)* …but you're not *wandering*, friend. i've *wardened* this *light* a *long* *while*, and i know the *look* of a soul that *came* for a *reason.* …*tell* me the *reason*, and i'll tell you if the *one* you're *looking* for …is *here.*

nodes.speaker: Sister Auria
nodes.text: *(She waits, patient as the light, and you understand that this gentle woman is, in her warm way, a gate — not to keep you out, but to be sure of what you carry before she points you toward the child in the gold.)* …there's a *girl* here you're *looking* for. *(it isn't a question)* …i can *see* it. *Anwen.* …she came to us *young* and *clean*, *commended* off the *grey* *rolls* with a *note* that her *household* was *tainted* and *she* alone *judged* *pure.* …a *sad* little line, that. a girl *arriving* *alone* in the *light* with her *whole* *family* marked *heretic* *behind* her. …*(her eyes sharpen, kindly)* …so before i send a *cold* thing to the *warmest* soul in this *house* — *tell* me, cold one. what *word* do you *carry* …and is it one that will *warm* her …or *break* her?

choices.text: *(read the warden — past the creed, to what she already suspects about the girl's sad little line)* "Hold a moment, Sister. Let me look at you — at what you've wondered about Anwen's note, all this time."

*(the one the grey kept, standing in the gold)* "I'll tell you what a cold soul wants in the dawn, Sister. I'm the one the Wall let back out — the one soul who walks the grey and the gold both. I carry words the dead can't carry themselves. I have one for Anwen. Let me through."

*(the whole shape of it, gathered at the Wall)* "The word is this, Sister: Anwen's household was never tainted. Her mother climbed into the Wall on a lie — confessed a heresy that was never hers — to strike the mark off her daughter and buy this child her place in your light. The note that brought Anwen here is a mother's forgery, written in her own damnation. I came to tell the girl what was paid for her."
"I'm not certain yet what I'll say to her, Sister. Let me sit in the light a while first."

nodes.speaker: Sister Auria
nodes.text: *(She holds still in the gold, and your sense moves past the dawn-creed to the quiet heresy this warden has never spoken.)* *(And you find a woman who has tended Anwen for years and has never once believed the note that brought her. Your sense reads the long private doubt of her: that a girl this good, this whole, this gently raised, does not come from a household of heretics — that you can read a childhood in a soul the way you read rings in a tree, and Anwen's rings are all warmth, all care, all a mother's patient hand, and no house that produced her was ever godless to its core. And your sense reads what Auria has done with that doubt, which is the thing that shames her: nothing. She has filed it, the way the grey files things, told herself it was not her place to question a roll-note from the courts of the dead, and let Anwen go on believing her family shamed her, because correcting it would mean opening a wound in a happy child and the warden's whole creed is the keeping of children from wounds. And under it your sense reads the thing she has prayed for without daring to name it: that someone would come who knew the truth of that note, so that she would not have to choose between a comfortable lie and a wound she has no right to open — so that the choosing would be taken out of her gentle, cowardly, loving hands.)*

nodes.speaker: Sister Auria
nodes.text: *(She looks at you the way the devout look at a miracle they were taught to expect and never thought to actually meet.)* …you *walk* them *both.* …the *grey* and the *gold.* …*(she breathes)* …then you are the *thing* this *house* has *no* *prayer* for, cold one, because we *pray* for the *dead* to *reach* the *light* and *never* once for the *light* to *reach* *back* into the *dark.* …*(she steps aside, toward the warm patch where the girl sits)* …*go* to her. …*Anwen's* by the *east* *candles.* …but cold one — *(her hand catches your sleeve, warm against your chill)* …*gently.* whatever you *carry.* she has had a *kind* *eternity.* …don't let the *grey* you *bring* …be the *first* *cold* thing she's *ever* *felt.*

nodes.speaker: Sister Auria
nodes.text: *(You give her the shape of it, and you watch a doubt she has carried for years turn, at last, into a knowledge she can no longer set down — and the relief in her face is terrible and total.)* …*a* *mother's* *forgery.* …*written* in her *own* *damnation.* …*(she sits down hard on the edge of the fountain, the warden's composure gone)* …i *knew.* …gods *forgive* me, i *always* *knew* that *note* was *wrong*, and i *filed* it, and i let that *child* spend *years* *ashamed* of the *one* soul who — *(she stops, presses her hands to her face, gathers herself)* …*no.* no, *now* is not the time for *my* *guilt; that's* a *warden's* *vanity*, to make the *girl's* *grief* about *my* *conscience.* …*(she stands, steadied by purpose)* …*go* to her, cold one. *tell* her. and tell her — *(her voice breaks and holds)* …tell her the *Dawn* *warden* who *raised* her *should* have *told* her *years* ago, and was too *gentle* and too *cowardly*, and is *sorry.* …she *deserves* the *truth* …and the *one* who *brings* it *should* not be a *stranger* …but it *has* to be *you*, because *you're* the one who *carried* it *across.* …*go.*

nodes.speaker: Sister Auria
nodes.text: *(She nods, and returns to her candles, tending each small flame with the care of a soul who has decided that keeping little lights is a life's worth of work.)* …*sit*, then. …the *light* *here* doesn't *cost* anything, cold one — *rest* in it as *long* as you *like.* …*(a soft glance toward the east candles)* …she'll be *there* when you're *ready.* …she's *always* *there.* …that's the *mercy* and the *sorrow* of a *kind* *eternity* both — *nothing* in it ever *has* to *change.* …not even a *girl* who's *wrong* about her *mother.*

conversations.title: Anwen — the daughter in the dawn

variants.text: *(She studies you, and the curious tilt of her head deepens into something almost knowing — as though the warmth in her can feel the shape of what you crossed three worlds to bring.)* …you came a *long* way, didn't you. …*(she says it softly)* …i can *tell.* the *cold* on you isn't the *thin* cold of someone who *wandered* in. it's the *deep* cold of someone who walked the *Wall* on *purpose.* …*(she folds her hands in her lap, brave and small)* …people don't walk the *Wall* on *purpose* …unless they're *carrying* something *from* it. …*say* it, cold one. i've been *warm* a *long* time. …i think i can *bear* one *cold* thing, if you've come all this *way* to *hand* it to me.

*(She looks at the bare place where a god's mark would sit, and instead of the flinch the wardens give it, she only softens.)* …you've no *god* on you. …*(not a judgment — a recognition)* …they *teach* us to *pity* the *Faithless*, here. …but you *crossed* into the *light* *anyway*, *unclaimed*, *cold* — and that's not a thing to *pity*, that's a thing to *wonder* at. …*(she picks the flower-chain back up, then sets it down again, restless)* …my *mother* was *Faithless.* …did you *know* that? is that *why* you've come — because you *knew* her *kind*? …*(quietly)* …i don't talk about her *much.* …*ask*, if you *want.* you've an *honest* *cold* face.

*(She watches you with the fearless ease of a soul that has never been hurt, and there is something almost unbearable in it — the brightness of a child who does not yet know the price of her own warmth.)* …i *like* you. *(she decides it plainly)* …most souls who pass through are so *sad*, and you're sad *too*, but you're sad in a way that *does* something with it. …*(she resumes her weaving)* …*talk* to me, cold one. it's a *long* *eternity* and you're the *first* *new* thing in it for *ages.* …*ask* me *anything.* i've *all* the *time* there *is.*

nodes.speaker: Anwen
nodes.text: *(She weaves, and talks, and the awful thing is how *content* she is — a settled, finished grief long since folded away, a girl who made her peace with a story that is a lie.)* …i'll *tell* you about me, if you *like*, since you're *carrying* a *quiet.* …i came here *young.* the *court* *note* said my *household* was *tainted* — my *father's* *doubt*, my *mother's* with it — and i alone came up *clean.* …*(her weaving slows, just slightly)* …i don't *light* the *remembrance* *candle* for them. the *rite*, every *year* — you light a flame for the *dead* you *love.* …i *don't.* …because how do you *love* a *mother* who threw the *family's* *god* away and let the *Wall* take her and *shamed* me into the *bargain? …(*she says it like a wound worn smooth)* …i *made* my *peace* with it. she *chose* the *dark.* …i *chose* not to *grieve* a *choice* like that.

choices.text: *(read the girl who made her peace with a lie — past the smooth-worn wound, to whether the peace is real)* "Let me look at you a moment, Anwen. At the peace you've made. I want to see if it's the real kind."

*(the one who walks both ways, and what that means here)* "I am the soul the Wall let back out, Anwen. I walk the grey and the gold both — which means I carry things between them that no one else can. Words. From the dark. To the light. Do you understand what I'm telling you I can do?"
"Why won't you light the candle, Anwen? Truly. Is it that you don't love her — or that loving her, the way you understand her, hurts too much to do on purpose once a year?"

*(the truth, carried three worlds — that the heresy was a coat, worn in the cold so this girl could stay warm)* "Anwen. Listen to me, and don't look away. The note lied. Your mother was not a heretic — she confessed a doubt that was never hers, climbed into the Wall on a forgery, took the whole taint onto her own name so that yours would come up clean. She didn't throw a god away. She threw *herself* away. So you could stand in this light. The heresy was a coat she put on in the cold — so you could stay warm."
"Keep your peace a while longer, Anwen. I'll come back."

nodes.speaker: Anwen
nodes.text: *(She lets you look, trusting as morning, and your sense slips past the settled surface to the truth the surface was built over.)* *(And you find the peace is not a lie, exactly, but a scar — a wound that healed crooked because it was set wrong. Your sense reads a girl who, at fourteen and newly dead and utterly alone in a strange gold light, was handed a single fact — your family was tainted, you alone are clean — and had to build an entire understanding of her own mother out of that one cruel sentence, with no one to correct it and forever to live in it. And it reads what that did: that to survive being the only one of her household to reach the light, she had to make her mother into someone who *deserved* to be left in the dark, because the alternative — that her mother was good, was loving, and went to the Wall anyway — was a grief too large for a fourteen-year-old soul to hold alone. So she chose the smaller pain. She chose anger. She decided her mother chose the dark, because a mother who *chose* it is easier to not-grieve than a mother who was *taken.* And your sense reads, under the anger, under the smooth-worn peace, the thing that has never once stopped: that every year when the remembrance rite comes and she does not light the candle, some part of her stands at the unlit wick and *aches*, and tells itself the ache is principle, and knows — in the deepest warm root of her — that the ache is *love*, the love she will not let herself feel for a woman she has decided to be ashamed of, because feeling it would mean the loss is real, and she has spent an eternity making very sure it is not.)*

nodes.speaker: Anwen
nodes.text: *(For the first time the fearless brightness in her stills, and something very old and very young looks out at you at once.)* …you carry things …*between* them. …*(her hands have gone quiet on the chain)* …from the *dark.* …to the *light.* …*(she swallows)* …i know what you're *telling* me you can *do*, cold one. you're telling me you could carry a word *from* …*(she can't quite say it)* …from *someone* in the *grey.* …*(and then, fast, a door slamming on a hope too big to look at)* …but there's *no* one in the *grey* for *me.* my *household* was *tainted.* they *chose* the *dark.* there's no *word* coming *from* there i *want* to — …*(her voice wavers, betraying her)* …*say* what you *came* to *say*, cold one. *plainly.* …i find i can't *breathe* around the *edges* of it.

nodes.speaker: Anwen
nodes.text: *(The question lands somewhere the smooth-worn story doesn't quite cover, and for a moment the girl underneath shows through.)* …*(she is quiet a long time)* …both. …*(barely)* …i tell *everyone* — i tell *myself* — that i don't *light* it because she doesn't *deserve* it. …*(she touches the unlit east candle without seeming to notice she's done it)* …but the *truth* i don't *say* …is that i'm *afraid* that if i *light* it *once* …if i let the *flame* stand for her for even a *breath* …i'll *feel* how much i — …*(she pulls her hand back)* …how much i *miss* a *woman* i've spent an *eternity* being *angry* at. and then the *anger* won't *hold* the *grief* *off* anymore. …and the *anger* is *all* that's been *holding* it. …*(she looks at you, raw)* …why are you *asking* me this, cold one? …*nobody* *asks* me this. …who *are* you to walk in *here* and *ask* me the *one* thing i —

nodes.speaker: Anwen
nodes.text: *(You say it plainly, and you do not let her look away, and you watch an eternity of crooked-healed peace break open along the wrong-set seam — and underneath it is not the catastrophe she spent so long bracing for. It is just the grief. The true-sized, true-shaped grief, the one she was always meant to carry, finally let in.)* …a *coat.* …*(she is shaking)* …she put on the *heresy* …like a *coat* …in the *cold* …so i could — …*(and then the anger she has held for an eternity simply lets go, all at once, because the thing it was bracing against was never true, and the grief floods into the space it leaves, and it is enormous, and it is *clean*)* …*Mother.* …oh, *Mother*, i've been so — i told everyone you *chose* the — and you *didn't*, you *never*, you *bought* me this *light* with your *whole* — …*(she turns, and with hands that have refused this for an eternity, she lifts a taper to the warm air and lights the east candle — the remembrance flame, at last, for Wessel)* …*there.* …it's *lit.* …it's been *lit* the whole *time*, hasn't it, in the part of me the *anger* covered. …*(she looks at you through the tears, and the smile that comes up is the realest thing in the gold)* …you said you carry *words* *across.* …*(fierce, certain)* …then carry her *this*, cold one. tell my *mother* …*Anwen* *lit* the *candle.* …tell her *Anwen* *knows.* …tell her the *coat* *worked* …and the *girl* …is *warm* …and she is *loved*, she is *loved*, she was *always* — …*carry* that to the *Wall.* …*please.* …*let* her *hear* it before the *stone* takes the *rest* of her.

nodes.speaker: Anwen
nodes.text: *(You find the exact words — not the blunt truth but the *true* one, the coat and the cold and the whole shape of a love that damned itself on purpose — and it does not break her, it *unbinds* her, an eternity of misset peace dissolving into something with light in it. And as the grief comes, the warm gold of the dawn-house seems to lean toward her, the way light leans toward what it was made for.)* …*(she does not flinch from it, does not crumble — she rises, and she is luminous with it)* …she *bought* me this. …every *morning* i've *had* here, every *warm* hour, she *paid* for with her *own* *forever.* …and i spent it being *ashamed* of her. …*(she lifts the taper, and lights not one candle but every wick along the east wall, a whole blaze of remembrance, and the warden Auria, across the hall, goes still and then weeps to see it)* …*let* the *whole* *Dawn* *see* it. …*Wessel* of the *Wall*, *Faithless* and *beloved*, *mother* who wore the *dark* like a *coat* — *remembered*, in the *House* of the *Light*, by the *daughter* she *saved.* …*(she turns to you, blazing and weeping and finally, wholly herself)* …*carry* it *back*, cold one. tell her *Anwen* *knows*, and *Anwen* *lit* the *Dawn* for her, and that wherever the *stone* finally *takes* her — she does not go *unmourned.* …she goes *lit.* …*go* *fast.* …*love* should not have to *wait* on *slow* *feet.*

nodes.speaker: Anwen
nodes.text: *(The truth comes out of you true but too fast, too much at once, and a soul that has spent an eternity armored in anger does the thing armor does — it holds.)* …*no.* …*no*, that's — you can't just *walk* in here and *unmake* the *one* thing i — …*(she is on her feet, the chain fallen, trembling)* …if that's *true* — if she *didn't* — then i spent an *eternity* being *ashamed* of someone who *died* for me, and i *can't* — i can't have *done* that, i *can't* have been *wrong* that *long* — …*(she presses her hands over her ears, a child again)* …*go.* please *go.* …i'm not — i *can't* — *(softer, breaking)* …say it *slower*, cold one. …*come* *back* and say it *slower.* …i *want* it to be *true* so *badly* it's *going* to *break* me, and i need you to *break* it to me *gently* …not all at *once* …please.

nodes.speaker: Anwen
nodes.text: *(She gathers the fallen flower-chain with shaking hands, not to weave it, just to hold something.)* …*come* *back.* …i *mean* it — *come* *back* and *tell* me again, when i've — when i can *stand* it. …*(she looks at the unlit east candle, then away)* …you've put a *crack* in an *eternity* of *peace*, cold one, and i think — i think it was a *peace* that *needed* *cracking*, but it's *cracked* *now* and i'm *standing* in the *draft* of it. …*(a wet, frightened, hopeful breath)* …if it's *true* …if she *did* that …then i've a *candle* to *light* and a *lifetime* of being *wrong* to *set* *down.* …*give* me a *moment* to be *brave* enough. …and *don't* — *don't* go *back* to the *Wall* and tell her i *turned* away. …tell her i'm *coming.* …tell her i just need a *breath.*

nodes.speaker: Anwen
nodes.text: *(You reach for the truth and grab the blunt edge of it, the fact without the love around it — *your mother lied, you were wrong, she damned herself for you* — and it lands not as a gift but as an indictment, and the warm girl recoils from it like a slap.)* …*why* would you — …*(she is white, stricken)* …why would you walk into the *one* *warm* place left in *all* of *death* and *tell* me i've spent an *eternity* *hating* a *saint*? …*what* is that *for*? …*(angry now, the anger that armored her turned on you)* …if it's *true* you've made my whole *peace* a *crime*, and if it's a *cruelty* you've — …*get* *out.* …*get* the *cold* *out* of my *light.* …*(and then, beneath the fury, the thing you bungled, leaking through)* …*(if it's true come back and say it like it's a* love *and not a* verdict, *because i can't take it as a* verdict, *i've passed enough of those on myself)*

nodes.speaker: Anwen
nodes.text: *(She nods, and goes back to her weaving, and the warm content settles over her again like a blanket over something that was, for just a moment, almost awake.)* …*alright.* …*(she threads a dawn-flower, calm again, or the shape of calm)* …i'll be *here.* i'm *always* *here.* …*(a small wave of the half-made chain)* …come *talk* to me whenever the *eternity* gets *long*, cold one. …i *like* the *cold* you *bring.* it's *honest.* …most things in the *gold* …aren't, *quite.*
